title: `RawInput` response field of container delete libpod API not documented
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Hi,
the [libpod API endpoint](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/containers/operation/ContainerDeleteLibpod) to delete a container does not document the `RawInput` field of the response. This might have been introduced in #15421 but was not documented in that PR.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ```podman system service unix:///home/`whoami`/testing.sock --log-level=debug --time=500```

2. `podman container create --name test_container testcontainer:latest`

3. ```curl -X DELETE --unix-socket /home/`whoami`/testing.sock http://d/v4.3.0/libpod/containers/test_container```

**Describe the results you received:**
`[{"Id":"3fcb8722a0730a83da14cdaa5602eb26885af166636cc44977194705587e4f1a","RawInput":"test_container"}]`

**Describe the results you expected:**
`[{"Id":"3fcb8722a0730a83da14cdaa5602eb26885af166636cc44977194705587e4f1a"}]`


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Fri Oct 21 10:16:35 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @arctic-alpaca!

@sstosh can you take a look?
I don't think this field should be leaked into the API at all. This is used for the cli command output only.

> I don't think this field should be leaked into the API at all. This is used for the cli command output only.

I think it's worth documenting exactly that. At the very least to avoid confusion.

If there is a possibility that we are asked the same question such as this issue in the future,
we should add the description about `RawInput`.
I would like to add the description if necessary.
I agree. Feel free to open a PR, thanks @sstosh
